stories.title: Orbán vows to keep fighting EU on first Brussels visit since election defeat
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was in a defiant mood on his return to Brussels on Wednesday, insisting that he’s staying in politics and will continue to fight the EU on issues such as migration. Speaking to a small group of reporters overlooking the European Commission’s Berlaymont headquarters, Orbán said his “goal has not changed” when it comes to the EU. “We have to reform Brussels … It’s very difficult to find a way which pr

stories.title: Senior European Council official opens communication channel with Kremlin
stories.summary: A member of European Council President António Costa’s cabinet reached out to open a channel of communication with the Kremlin, an EU official told POLITICO. The direct outreach — a break with a de facto EU policy of not speaking directly to Russia since it launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022 — comes as European leaders debate opening direct talks with Moscow about ending the war. The contacts, which the official said happened in recent weeks, were described as brief and un

stories.title: Germany won’t commit to Hormuz mission before seeing US-Iran deal
stories.summary: BERLIN — German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul urged the U.S. and Iran to make public the details of their peace agreement on Wednesday, saying Berlin could only consider joining a mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz once the terms are clear. The U.S. and Iran are due to sign their memorandum of understanding toward ending over three months of conflict in the Middle East on Friday. The document hasn’t yet been released, leaving questions as to its exact provisions and whether the agreement

stories.title: Germany news: Ministers discuss refugee deportation rules
stories.summary: Germany's regional interior ministers are holding their biannual conference with immigration, deportations and benefits high on the agenda. Meanwhile, Germany and Poland are marking 35 years of cooperation. DW has more.

stories.title: Orbán backs ‘legend’ Le Pen over Bardella as French far right presidential candidate
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Former Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán hopes Marine Le Pen is the far-right candidate in next year’s French presidential election. Speaking to journalists during his first visit to Brussels since losing power in April, Orbán declined to say if he would campaign for Jordan Bardella, the 30-year-old MEP who looks poised to be the National Rally’s candidate next year. Instead, Orbán threw his support behind Le Pen, the far-right party’s leader in the French parlia

stories.title: Pauline Hanson makes clear what a One Nation government would be like – it’s an ugly picture
stories.summary: The One Nation leader offered a series of inflammatory statements at the National Press Club that may play well with her supporters but will alarm many others Pauline Hanson says Australia ‘must be monocultural’ in National Press Club speech Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast For months, voters have been telling anyone who’ll listen that they want Pauline Hanson. Pollsters , journalists and the (current) political establishment have all heard how the major parties are l

stories.title: ‘We had right of way’: when British fair play met Russian firepower off the Isle of Wight
stories.summary: Jane and Alan Kelvey’s holiday yacht met a warship in the Channel – at a tense time for Anglo-Russian relations “We actually had right of way,” said Jane Kelvey, a little crossly, though keeping it civil. “But we weren’t going to argue with a warship.” The dramatic standoff in the Channel on Tuesday morning between Admiral Grigorovich, a 125-metre (409ft) battle-hardened Russian frigate, and Bright Future, a 12-metre (40ft) pleasure yacht owned and helmed by Jane, 69, and her husband, Alan, 71,

stories.title: Atlantic hurricane season opens with Tropical Storm Arthur inundating Gulf coast
stories.summary: The southern US is seeing intense rain that is expected to cause dangerous flash flooding Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The first tropical storm of the Atlantic hurricane season formed on Wednesday near the Gulf coast, bringing intense rain and the threat of dangerous flash floods to states including Texas and Louisiana , meteorologists said. Tropical Storm Arthur was a disorganized cluster of storms that brought rain for days over parts of eastern Mexico and the Gulf. The Na

stories.title: Sierra Leone’s first lady refuses to condemn FGM without ‘reliable data’ on harms
stories.summary: Exclusive: health professionals, survivors and politicians voice concerns in open letter over comments by Fatima Maada Bio, who denies supporting the practice The first lady of Sierra Leone has denied that she supports female genital mutilation amid rising anger around her perceived approval of the practice. But in an exclusive response to the Guardian, Fatima Maada Bio, the wife of President Julius Maada Bio, also said she would not openly condemn FGM until she saw “reliable data” that the prac

stories.title: Germany World Cup shirts run out of letter V
stories.summary: Bad news for fans of Germany players Kai Havertz, Deniz Undav and Aleksandar Pavlovic: the kit makers have run out of the letter V, such is the demand for those names on shirts. But don't worry, more Vs are on the way!

stories.title: Orbán in Brussels: "The advance of patriotic forces continues, Brussels needs to be reformed"
stories.summary: The former prime minister is attending the meeting of the PfE group in Brussels. At a press conference, he spoke about his thoughts on an internal dispute within Fidesz, their expectation that the new government not leave a single penny of Hungarian funds with the EU, and the World Cup.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: “I hated the whole thing anyway. And Fidesz, too" – we spoke with fired employees amidst mass layoffs at Fidesz's propaganda hub
stories.summary: The media company that served as the propaganda arm of the Orbán government is laying off 200 people. Outside company headquarters, those being laid off spoke about compromises they had made, keeping their mouths shut when things got uncomfortable, and feeling ashamed of their workplace—as well as the fact that many of them had voted for Tisza.

stories.title: Már mérhető eredménye lehet a dohányzás elleni fellépésnek: látványosan javultak a daganatos adatok
stories.summary: A 40–59 éves magyaroknál több mint 60 százalékkal csökkent a dohányzással összefüggő daganatok előfordulása, de a kutatók szerint ez csak közvetetten köthető össze a dohányzás visszaszorulásával.

stories.title: Túl jól hangzik, hogy igaz legyen: 3,5 kilométernyi zöld Duna-parti sétány lehet Újbudán
stories.summary: A Pünkösdfürdő park után újabb nagy Duna-parti zöldfejlesztés indulhat hamarosan Budapesten, ezúttal Buda délebbi részén, 3,5 kilométer hosszan, a Kopaszi-gáttól egészen le Budafokig. A részletes koncepcióterveket kedden mutatták be. Megnéztük, mi van most, és mi lesz majd a helyszínen, ha minden a tervek szerint alakul.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely szerint jogszerűen jártak el a magyar állampolgárságot kapó kanadaiak esetében
stories.summary: Velkey György László korábban arról beszélt, egy Gulyás minisztériumához köthető magyar köztisztviselő is aktívan részt vett abban, hogy olyan kanadaiak jussanak magyar állampolgársághoz, akiket semmilyen valódi szál nem köt Magyarországhoz.

stories.title: Bige Orbánnak: „Ön ma már egy senki!” Orbán Bigének: „Megértettem, hogy nehéz az oligarchák élete”
stories.summary: A Nitrogénművek tulajdonosa nyílt levelet írt Orbán Viktornak, aki nyílt levélben válaszolt is neki.

stories.title: Remains of Hungarian King Béla II found, with two more relatives from House of Árpád also identified
stories.summary: Most royal tombs had been disturbed over the course of history, and the bones were then collected in an ossuary.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor Brüsszelben: A patrióta erők előretörése folytatódik, Brüsszelt meg kell reformálni
stories.summary: A volt kormányfő a Patrióták brüsszeli találkozóján vesz részt. A sajtótájékoztatón beszélt egy belső pártvitára adott reflexióról, a foci-vb-n való részvételéről, és arról, hogy azt várják el az új kormánytól, hogy egyetlen fillért se hagyjanak az EU-nál.

stories.title: Ártatlannak vallotta magát a Till Tamás megölésével vádolt férfi az első tárgyalási napon
stories.summary: A korábban tett, egymásnak ellentmondó vallomásaiból azt tartotta fenn a bíróság előtt, miszerint a gyerek baleset áldozata lett. Azt szeretné, ha a bíróság újra megvizsgálná az ügy elévülésének kérdését.

stories.title: Surányi György: Az euró bevezetése mellett is lehet pocsék gazdaságpolitikát folytatni
stories.summary: Az MTA-n a volt jegybankelnök és László Csaba volt pénzügyminiszter is beszélt a Magyarország előtt álló nehézségekről.

stories.title: Az egyik legfontosabb jóléti mutató szerint Magyarország még mindig az EU egyik legszegényebb országának számít
stories.summary: 2024-ben is a rangsor végén voltunk. Magyarország adata a gazdasági fejlettségi szintjéhez viszonyítva is alacsonynak számít.

stories.title: „Nem vagyok modell, mit magyarázkodjak?” – mondta Marcelo Bielsa a cipőbámulós vb-portréjáról
stories.summary: Az uruguayi válogatott edzője hosszasan méltatlankodott, amikor arról kérdezték, miért néz zsebre tett kézzel lefelé a róla készült hivatalos képen.

stories.title: L. Simon László: Nem létezik Fidesz-akarat, olyan van, hogy Orbán Viktor-i koncepció
stories.summary: Reméli, megmarad a Magyar Művészeti Akadémia, nem tudja, ki az a Cooky, Grecsó Krisztiánnak pedig azt üzeni, hogy nagyon szégyellje magát.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor szerint Hatvanpuszta műbalhé, ez a hatékony és igaz válasz
stories.summary: A volt miniszterelnök Brüsszelben tartott sajtótájékoztatót, a korrupcióval kapcsolatos kérdésekre nem válaszolt, azt viszont elárulta, hogy van jegye a foci-vb döntőjére.

stories.title: HVG: A kanadai Magyar Diaszpóra Tanács elnöke kérvényezett állampolgárságot tehetős kanadaiaknak
stories.summary: A HVG megkérdezte azt a két embert, akikre Velkey György László utalt, amikor arról beszélt, hogy kanadaiaknak intéztek pénzért magyar állampolgárságot.

stories.title: Kezdeményezi a benzinárstop kivezetését a kormány
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerint a rögzített ár alá csökkent a piaci ár, ezért nem indokolt tovább fenntartani.

stories.title: Intő jel Putyinnak, hogy Trump az európaiakkal milyen egyetértésben állt ki Ukrajna mellett
stories.summary: A G7-csúcs közös nyilatkozata kedvez a Trumppal tárgyaló Zelenszkijnek és Ukrajnának, ehhez pedig az iráni helyzet is hozzájárulhatott.

stories.title: Kongó meglepte a portugálokat, történetének első gólja mellé az első pontját is megszerezte a vb-n
stories.summary: Portugália hat perc után előnybe került, de innentől teljesen ötlettelenné vált a játéka, Yoane Wissa pedig egy szép fejesgóllal egyenlített az első félidő végén. A vége is 1-1 lett, ezzel Kongó történelmet írt.

stories.title: Sikeresen pályáztak az előző kormány kkv-támogató programjára, hónapok óta hiába várnak a hitelre
stories.summary: A Gazdasági és Energetikai Minisztérium azt közölte, a programot jelenleg felülvizsgálja a gyökeresen átalakított Magyar Fejlesztési Bank, ez várhatóan még ebben a hónapban lezárulhat.

stories.title: The Guardian view on defending Europe in a new era: collaboration is the key | Editorial
stories.summary: The recent abandonment of plans for a Franco-German fighter jet sent a disastrous signal. Strategic autonomy will be jointly achieved or not at all It has become a truism to assert that Europe needs to fast-track its own strategic independence in a volatile world. A recent paper from the European Council on Foreign Relations describes the continent’s leaders as grappling with “a ‘Schrödinger’s NATO’ moment, in which America remains formally inside the alliance while behaving as though it were no

stories.title: How Germany and Poland rebooted relations 35 years ago
stories.summary: When Germany and Poland signed the Treaty on Good Neighborliness, Friendship and Cooperation in June 1991, they chose partnership over enmity and became close NATO and EU allies. But it hasn't all been plain sailing.

stories.title: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.title: EU, Ukraine start formal accession talks after Orban delay
stories.summary: The EU has formally opened the first stage of accession negotiations with Ukraine. This comes two years later than planned, as former Hungarian PM Viktor Orban was blocking the process prior to his election defeat.

stories.title: US lawmakers demand Trump officials halt plan to send Afghans to DRC
stories.summary: Government urged to reconsider proposal for 1,100 Afghans, currently stranded in Qatar, who worked with US forces Dozens of US lawmakers urged the Trump administration on Thursday to roll back any plans to ship to unsafe third countries Afghan nationals who worked with US forces during the war in their homeland. In a letter seen by Reuters, more than 80 House of Representatives members, including at least three Republicans as well as Democrats, appealed to secretary of state, Marco Rubio, to rec

stories.title: ‘Regrettable’: Republican senator breaks with Trump after president delays Jay Clayton’s nomination for intelligence director – live
stories.summary: Senate intelligence committee chair Tom Cotton says hearing postponed after Trump ordered Jay Clayton to not appear; delay extends lapse of powerful US surveillance tool Trump derails confirmation process for Jay Clayton Sign up for the Breaking News US email Donald Trump also denied (again) that the memorandum of understanding includes a $300bn fund for Iran , and denied that he had asked the Gulf states to commit funding. “It’s false,” Trump told reporters as he sat alongside Egyptian presiden

stories.title: West plays nice on AI in bid to shut out China
stories.summary: EVIAN-LES-BAINS, France — The West should come together to rein in the most capable artificial intelligence models to keep China at bay, leaders and top AI CEOs agreed in talks on Wednesday. As leaders of the seven richest countries spent more than two hours around a table with 11 of the richest men in the world, there was little to show that Washington had just suspended access to Anthropic’s latest models , creating tensions between the U.S. and its allies, while throwing America’s own AI poli

stories.title: Trump again slams Netanyahu’s campaign in Lebanon
stories.summary: President Donald Trump lit into Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday, underscoring his frustrations with the key ally’s continued military campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon even as he looks to sell a peace deal the U.S. negotiated with the group’s main benefactor, Iran. “We have a little dispute over Lebanon,” he told reporters at a G7 press conference. “I say, ‘You can do a little softer touch, Bibi. You don’t have to knock down a building every time somebody walks into it

stories.title: Construction equipment multinationals may be aiding Israeli war crimes, experts say
stories.summary: Exclusive: Images show Israeli military using six companies’ bulldozers and excavators to demolish south Lebanon villages Human rights experts have alleged that six multinational construction equipment conglomerates may be aiding and abetting war crimes by supplying excavators and bulldozers to Israel, after photos and videos showed the Israeli military using their equipment to demolish villages in south Lebanon. The Guardian geolocated and verified images showing the Israeli military using exca

stories.title: Burnham says he wants to ensure Makerfield byelection ‘changes British politics’, in eve-of-poll speech – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog has now closed. See all our UK politics coverage here Andy Burnham may have trouble getting through to Keir Starmer if he tries ringing him after the Makerfield byelection to urge him to set a timetable for his departure. Burnham reportedly wants to call Starmer this weekend. (See 9.47am .) But, in his interview with Sky News, Starmer said: “I’m sure I’ll talk to Andy after the weekend.” If Starmer declines to take Burnham’s call, he may be following Ed Miliband’s example. In a Times s

stories.summary: This blog has now closed – see all our coverage of the G7 summit here Rutte says the adjustment in the US pledge to the Nato Force Model is “not primarily about where forces and assets are currently, but about who would do what if our defence plans were activated.” He says historically the model was “overly reliant” on the US. “You will likely have seen news adjusting its contributions to the Nato force model. In some cases, this has been cast as a problem, as the US pulling away from its allies

stories.title: French police authorised to use water cannon on asylum seekers in £660m deal with UK
stories.summary: Refugee charity says decision to let riot police use water cannon, which are banned in Great Britain, is ‘sickening’ French riot police deployed in northern France under a £660m deal with the UK are authorised to use water cannon against asylum seekers, the Guardian has been told. Two specialist policing units, including a 50-officer riot squad, have begun working to prevent asylum seekers and people smugglers from launching small boats under the UK-France deal in time for the summer months. Con

stories.title: Is Russia heading for a fuel crisis?
stories.summary: Russia's fuel output is falling after Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries. Seasonal spikes in gasoline and diesel demand could push the system into a deeper crisis, experts say.

stories.title: EU lawmakers approve new tougher migrant policy
stories.summary: The EU is backing tougher migration policies that seek to ramp up deportations of failed asylum-seekers. Under the new legislation, EU states can establish "return hubs."

stories.title: Putin meets with ASEAN leaders in Russia as G7 vow 'unwavering' support for Ukraine
stories.summary: It comes after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met with G7 leaders in France. Zelenskyy said on Wednesday they had agreed on the "additional strengthening of Ukraine’s air defense" and new measures against Moscow.

stories.title: Group planned to attack White House UFC event using snipers and drones, FBI says
stories.summary: In newly unsealed court filings, the government says the group allegedly expressed grievances about corruption, the Epstein files, and data centres.

stories.title: Ex-Nigeria oil minister cleared in UK bribery trial
stories.summary: Diezani Alison-Madueke had been accused of receiving bribes from oil tycoons but was found not guilty by a jury at Southwark Crown Court.

stories.title: Trump goes after critics who want a more comprehensive deal with Iran
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday lambasted his critics who accused him of caving to Iran, calling them “stupid” and arguing that prolonging the war could lead to an “international depression.” “There’s not one nation that came to us and said ‘please keep dropping bombs on them, please keep dropping bombs’ — the stupid people say that,” Trump said at the the close of the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France. “The one thing I didn’t want to see is, I didn’t want to see economic catastroph

stories.title: Seven-year-old Abdiqadir was hit in a US airstrike. Without a $750 operation, he may lose his ability to walk
stories.summary: Abdiqadir Salah was pierced by shrapnel in a bombing that killed 12 in Somalia. But as the US denies civilians were hurt they face no hope of compensation Read more: Killed walking home from school: why did Somali children become targets of US drone strikes? A seven-year-old boy who was riddled with shrapnel during a deadly US airstrike in Somalia faces losing his ability to walk unless he has a £750 emergency operation. But Abdiqadir Salah’s family cannot afford the surgery and the US – which r

stories.title: AFP investigates banner stunt during Pauline Hanson’s National Press Club speech
stories.summary: Activist group GetUp claims responsibility for incident, saying ‘we thought the occasion deserved some honesty’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian federal police is investigating a protest incident during Pauline Hanson’s speech at the National Press Club after political activist group GetUp claimed responsibility for the stunt. In a statement on Wednesday afternoon, the National Press Club said it had referred the incident to police after concluding none

stories.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
stories.summary: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Two children dead after pram hit by car in Sydney despite ‘phenomenal’ bystander efforts to save them
stories.summary: Police found two children – both aged under five – had been struck by a car in Cabramatta, in the city’s south-west Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two children in a pram have been struck and killed by a car in Sydney’s south-west. Emergency services were called to the intersection of Joseph and Gilbert streets, Cabramatta at about 3.15pm on Wednesday after reports of a crash, New South Wales police said. Continu

stories.title: ‘Period tax’ on sanitary products to be abolished, says Pakistan minister
stories.summary: Campaigners welcome announcement cutting levies on menstrual health items, but say their work to end period poverty is ‘far from over’ Pakistan plans to abolish “period tax”, in a victory for young campaigners who had taken the government to court over the charges. Finance minister Muhammad Aurangzeb announced that sanitary towels and related items were “daily necessities that are indispensable for women’s health, dignity and full participation in social activities”, and said he intended to remo

stories.title: Protesters to rally against World Cup sponsor Hyundai before Mexico game
stories.summary: Focus on business dealings with mining company Guadalajara rally to highlight fate of ‘disappeared’ Hyundai will be targeted by protesters at a rally before the Group A game between Mexico and South Korea in Guadalajara on Thursday, due to the World Cup sponsor’s business dealings with the South American mining company Ternium. A 2025 report from the environmental group Mighty Earth criticised Hyundai’s involvement in what they described as a “dirty steel supply chain”, as the South Korean motor

stories.title: Starmer warns against ‘looking backwards’ to Brexit after rivals back UK’s return to EU
stories.summary: Speaking at the G7 summit, prime minister says he stands by Labour’s manifesto pledge not to rejoin bloc Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates The UK and the European Union should not waste time “looking backwards” to Brexit, Keir Starmer said on Wednesday, as he comes under pressure to reconsider rejoining the EU. The prime minister reaffirmed his government’s manifesto commitment to not re-enter the bloc, but said there had been “real progress” with the relationship, w

stories.title: Forced adoption survivors to get full apology from UK government, says Phillipson
stories.summary: Education secretary describes historical practice in England as a ‘shameful period’ in country’s history Downing Street is to make a full apology on behalf of the state to those affected by historical forced adoption in England, the education secretary has confirmed. Bridget Phillipson, giving evidence to MPs on the education select committee on Wednesday, described it as a “shameful period” in UK history. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK officials expect Russia to retaliate for seizure of shadow fleet oil tanker
stories.summary: No formal warning issued to captains or ship owners, but industry body says they are exercising greater vigilance British officials believe Russia will try to retaliate for the Royal Marines’ seizure of the oil tanker Smyrtos, prompting UK ship owners to exercise greater vigilance until tensions with Moscow ease. Military sources said the UK had considered possible responses to the seizure of the vessel carrying Russian crude worth $40m (£30m) to India, and anticipate that the Kremlin will want

stories.title: Trump official declines to call January 6 an ‘attack’, describing ‘protests and such’
stories.summary: Don Berthiaume, Trump nominee for inspector general at justice department, pressed by senators amid confirmation Donald Trump ’s nominee to serve as a top independent watchdog at the Department of Justice has refused to call the January 6 insurrection an “attack” during questioning by US senators. Don Berthiaume, a career justice department employee who has been serving as inspector general at the agency, faced senators as part of his confirmation process to take up the role permanently on Wedne

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran agrees to destroy enriched uranium stockpile, memorandum read out by US officials says

stories.title: Ghana to advance reparatory justice at first major gathering since landmark UN resolution
stories.summary: Heads of state and participants from more than 80 countries at three-day event in Accra to pursue actionable commitments to reconciliation and restitution Ghana is hosting a conference to advance the continent’s push for reparatory justice after the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Heads of state and government, ministers, civil society representatives, historians, researchers and legal e

stories.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
stories.summary: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

stories.title: India: Telegram to challenge government order blocking app
stories.summary: Telegram has reportedly challenged the Indian government's order to temporarily block the popular messaging app ahead of the NEET medical entrance exam.

stories.title: NATO chief focuses on alliance unity after US alters pledges
stories.summary: NATO's secretary general went to great lengths to cast a positive light on the alliance ahead of a ministers' meeting. The closest he came to addressing any tension was saying members have "a lot to talk about."

stories.title: Diplomats and abuse — chipping at the shield of immunity
stories.summary: Domestic workers who were mistreated by diplomats are often denied justice due to their abusers enjoying diplomatic immunity. But court rulings in the UK and Switzerland have paved the way for change.

stories.title: 'They came with machetes' - deadline looms for migrants to leave South Africa
stories.summary: Protesters have set 30 June as the date for all undocumented migrants to leave the country.

stories.title: Bolivia signs $20m deal with US to fight drug trafficking, foreign ministry says
stories.summary: Under its new president, Bolivia is re-establishing severed ties with the US to combat organised crime.

stories.title: Iran sends tankers loaded with oil past US military blockade
stories.summary: Three Iranian tankers loaded with crude oil have passed the US blockade line in the Gulf of Oman, ship-tracking data shows.

stories.title: From regime change to regime deal? How Tehran's political camps are reacting to leaked US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Iran's hardliners have framed the framework deal as a victory, even as Trump dismissed reports of a $300bn reconstruction fund and warned he would resume strikes if Tehran did not comply.

stories.title: Mangione's lawyers plan psychiatric defence in state murder trial
stories.summary: They will argue he was suffering from "extreme emotional disturbance" at the time UnitedHealth CEO Brian Thompson was killed.

stories.title: Luigi Mangione to use psychiatric defence in health CEO murder trial, judge says
stories.summary: Brian Thompson was killed in December 2024 as he walked to a Manhattan hotel for UnitedHealth Group’s annual investor conference.

stories.title: Starmer, Modi agree to implement UK-India trade deal from July 15
stories.summary: LONDON — U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer and India’s Narendra Modi agreed on the sidelines of the G7 leaders summit to implement the trade deal they signed nearly a year ago. The two countries announced Wednesday that the deal will enter into force on July 15, after Starmer and Modi met in the spa town of Évian-les-Bains, France, on Tuesday evening. “We are bringing our landmark trade deal with India into force as quickly as we can, because we want businesses and the public to feel the benefits

stories.title: Meloni says Italian government won’t push for social media ban on kids
stories.summary: EVIAN-LES-BAINS, France — Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Wednesday said her government would not take the initiative to introduce a social media ban on teens like those in the works in France, the U.K and other countries. “I am not against a social media ban for under 16s, but I am not either convinced that this proposal alone can solve the problem because that type of ban can be easily circumvented,” Meloni told reporters at the end of a G7 summit. Stating that a ban risks “to partial

stories.title: Labour came to power with no big idea for relations with EU, says former top diplomat
stories.summary: Ivan Rogers, Britain’s EU ambassador from 2013 to 2017, says party’s ideas did not ‘remotely measure up’ to challenge UK politics live – latest updates Labour arrived in power with no big idea on the UK’s future relationship with the EU, a former British ambassador to Brussels has said. Ivan Rogers, Britain’s EU ambassador from 2013 to 2017, said Labour presented “a ragbag of issues” on the EU in its manifesto, which did not “remotely measure up to the challenge of the times” and would “make no

stories.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue read

stories.title: Real estate event in London ‘advertised sale of land in illegal Israeli settlements’
stories.summary: Pamphlets from event featured projects in West Bank and East Jerusalem despite previous denials by organisers An Israeli real estate event in north London appears to have advertised the sale of land in Israeli settlements in East Jerusalem and the occupied West Bank, despite previous denials that illegal settlement properties would be marketed at the event. Pamphlets shared with the Guardian from the event on Sunday showed real estate projects in Ma’ale Adumim, Givat Ze’ev, Kfar Eldad and Teneh

stories.title: ‘Very naive’: French left up in arms as Macron hosts Trump at Versailles
stories.summary: Political rivals accuse French president of grovelling after inviting US counterpart to Louis XIV’s palace The US president, Donald Trump , will be the guest of honour at a sumptuous dinner at the Palace of Versailles, as the French left criticises Emmanuel Macron for going too far in attempts to flatter him. “Versailles is not gold leaf, Versailles is the real deal,” said Trump of the opulent 2,300-room palace which was once home to France’s Sun King, Louis XIV. “I’m a fan of beautiful places.”

stories.title: Abandoned pony grows his strength in fourth floor flat – video
stories.summary: When Mile the pony was rejected by his mother at birth, his owner, Andjelka Josipovic, decided to take him in. Each night she carries him up to her fourth-floor flat in the Croatian town of Rovinj. Mile spends his days on a ranch but needs regular feeding and attention during the night. Josipovic hopes that after a few weeks he will be strong enough to stay on the ranch full time Continue reading...

stories.title: Pauline Hanson’s speech ‘shameful’ and echoed ‘rubbish’ from rightwing figures in UK and US, critics say
stories.summary: Labor minister says Australians would lose their jobs and wages would go down if One Nation’s policies became law Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has been roundly condemned for her National Press Club speech, with advocacy groups accusing the One Nation leader of using “hatred for political gain” while the Greens say Hanson is echoing “rubbish” lines from rightwing figures in the UK and US. In Hanson’s first address after 20 years in politics, the lead

stories.title: Ancient DNA provides evidence of earliest known plague outbreak
stories.summary: Discovery in Siberia suggests bacterium from raw marmots devastated hunter-gatherer tribes about 5,500 years ago The earliest evidence for an outbreak of plague has been uncovered at late stone age cemeteries in south-eastern Siberia where dozens of hunter-gatherers and their children were buried. Ancient DNA collected from the remains suggests the disease tore through the sparse communities in devastating waves that began about 5,500 years ago, at least two centuries after the bacterium respons

stories.title: Recently single Australian men are seven times more likely to report a suicide attempt, study shows
stories.summary: Fathers particularly affected, with almost 15% reporting suicidal plans after relationship breakdowns, according to survey of 20,000 men Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Men who have recently gone through a breakup are seven times more likely to report a suicide attempt than those who haven’t, a new study shows The Ten to Men project has followed more than 20,000 Australian men and identified the mental health risks in relationship breakdowns, which can include change

stories.title: Keir Starmer signals he would give Andy Burnham a cabinet job
stories.summary: PM says Greater Manchester mayor is ‘huge asset’ who can play big part in Labour government if he wins byelection UK politics live – latest updates Keir Starmer has indicated he would give Andy Burnham a cabinet job, describing him as a “huge asset”, as he attempted to head off a challenge to his leadership that is expected to come after the Makerfield byelection on Thursday. Allies of Burnham said the Greater Manchester mayor would not be interested in serving under Starmer if he returned to We

stories.title: Russia warship fires warning shot at British couple – The Latest
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has called the firing of warning shots by a Russian warship at a British yacht sailing across the Channel on Tuesday ‘deeply concerning and reckless’. Russia’s defence ministry said the yacht was on a ‘dangerous course’ and several attempts were made to contact it – a claim disputed by the retired couple onboard the 40ft yacht. Nosheen Iqbal speaks to the Guardian’s defence and security editor, Dan Sabbagh – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

stories.title: Macron hails US alignment with G7’s ‘shared commitment’ on Ukraine
stories.summary: French president welcomes group’s unity after ‘very deep change’ in Washington’s approach Emmanuel Macron has said the whole of the G7, including the US, recognises “the territorial integrity of Ukraine” as he hailed a “re-synchronisation” of positions on the issue. The French president welcomed a “very deep change in the US approach”, saying Donald Trump and all the leaders present at the G7 summit at Évian-les-Bains understood that Vladimir Putin was not interested in peace. Continue reading..

stories.title: Golden ticket: why Versailles invite is the ‘real deal’ for Trump
stories.summary: The US president’s opulent tastes will be well served in a palace that has hosted Putin, King Charles and JFK For a US president who has likened himself to a king and redecorated the Oval Office with golden paint and gold ornaments , it feels like the perfect dinner venue. Donald Trump said one of the deciding factors in accepting an invitation to dine with Emmanuel Macron at the spectacular, 2,300-room Palace of Versailles was that it was “not gold leaf” but the “real deal”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Titan sub: design flaws and company groupthink central to catastrophe, report finds
stories.summary: Canadian officials find structural defects in material used for hull and say firm failed to fully test ‘novel’ design Canadian safety officials have issued a damning report on the catastrophic final voyage of the Titan submersible, finding that the US company behind the expedition was overcome by “groupthink” and “confirmation bias” and failed to understand the profound risks confronting their largely untested craft. The 6.7-metre (22ft) carbon fibre submersible dipped below the surface of the A

stories.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady for fourth time this year
stories.summary: Open markets committee says ‘economic activity is expanding at a solid pace’ in first meeting under new chair Kevin Warsh The US Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged for the fourth time this year after its first meeting under new chair, Kevin Warsh, a Donald Trump appointee who has taken over the central bank during a tumultuous time for the US economy. “Economic activity is expanding at a solid pace despite elevated uncertainty that owes, in part, to the conflict in the Middle East,” t

stories.title: US releases text of Iran peace plan as Trump says deal averts ‘worldwide depression’

In extraordinary remarks on Wednesday, Donald Trump went from thr

stories.title: Burnham team tell ministers to delay resignations to avoid chaos
stories.summary: Exclusive: Allies of Labour’s Makerfield candidate concerned rapid collapse of Starmer government would increase instability Andy Burnham’s campaign has been forced to talk ministers out of resigning as early as this weekend to avoid Keir Starmer’s government descending into chaos amid fallout from the Makerfield byelection, the Guardian can reveal. As they prepare for a potential change of leader in the event he beats Reform on Thursday, Burnham’s team is increasingly concerned a rapid collapse

stories.title: Jair Bolsonaro’s son sentenced to four years in jail for seeking US interference in father’s Brazil coup trial
stories.summary: Brazil supreme court finds that Eduardo Bolsonaro – who resides in the US - tried to get sanctions put on judges trying ex-president over coup plot Brazil’s supreme court has sentenced Eduardo Bolsonaro to four years and two months in prison after finding him guilty of courting US ⁠interference in his father’s coup plot trial last year. The office of Brazil’s ‌prosecutor general had ‌charged Eduardo Bolsonaro – who lives in the US - courting interference from the Trump administration to help Jai

stories.title: Lib Dems to urge Labour to drop ‘torpor and timidity’ on EU and rejoin single market
stories.summary: Exclusive: Ed Davey to make call ahead of 10th anniversary of Brexit vote, in strengthening of party’s position on EU The Lib Dems will urge Andy Burnham to end Labour’s “torpor and timidity” towards the EU as they call for the UK to rejoin the single market, in a notable strengthening of their own position. Ahead of the 10th anniversary of the Brexit vote next week, Ed Davey will challenge Burnham to scrap Labour’s red lines on the customs union and single market if he becomes prime minister an

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
stories.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

stories.title: South Korea to shrink security buffer zone to North Korea
stories.summary: South Korea will ease restrictions on roughly 270 square kilometers of land near the border with North Korea. Farmers in the area had long complained of rules that complicated working conditions.

stories.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
stories.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

stories.title: Iran's hardliners fear being sidelined in US deal
stories.summary: As an Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war moves closer to formal signature, hardline supporters of the Islamic Republic fear losing influence as Iran's politics shift from maximalist defiance to compromise.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Africa's new era takes shape
stories.summary: From Cape Verde's debut to DR Congo's return, Africa's expanded presence at the 2026 World Cup brings new stories and renewed expectations of success.

stories.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
stories.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

stories.title: EU, G7 pledge support amid DRC Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: G7 leaders called for a "strong and coordinated response" to the latest Ebola outbreak. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen said that "health security is shared security." The recorded death toll in DRC is approaching 200.

stories.title: Middle East: Trump could restart Iran war if deal not signed
stories.summary: President Trump says the US would "start the process again" if Iran doesn't make a deal. Meanwhile, Lebanon's president said talks with Israel are independent and should not be linked to the US-Iran deal. Follow DW.

stories.title: Germany: Reports of antisemitism in 2025 remain high
stories.summary: The Federal Association of Departments for Research and Information on Antisemitism (RIAS) recorded over 8,700 cases they classify as antisemitic hostility in 2025, many of which were connected with the State of Israel.

stories.title: Javier Bardem joins Hollywood greats at landmark ceremony
stories.summary: Spanish film star Javier Bardem was honoured in Los Angeles on 16 June with a place in the celebrated forecourt of the TCL Chinese Theatre, a distinction reserved for some of cinema's biggest names.

stories.title: Status quo at Jerusalem's holiest site under threat as Israeli nationalists flout rules
stories.summary: Israeli nationalists are increasingly flouting a convention on how faiths share the al-Aqsa mosque compound.

stories.title: Thousands flock to Art Basel in Switzerland as market outlook improves
stories.summary: Art Basel 2026 in Basel draws thousands as high-end works by Picasso, Warhol and Hockney fuel a recovering global art market after years of decline.

stories.title: Equatorial Guinea government resigns after failing to meet targets
stories.summary: The vice-president says the government had barely reached 10% of its targets, without specifying them.

stories.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite Trump criticism
stories.summary: Speaking on Tuesday, Trump said Israel's PM Benjamin Netanyahu needed "to be more responsible with respect to Lebanon".

stories.title: Norway's crown princess undergoes successful lung transplant, palace says
stories.summary: Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffered from pulmonary fibrosis, will now spend several weeks recovering in hospital.

stories.title: Hundreds of cats rescued from being eaten in Vietnam
stories.summary: More than 500 cats were seized in Vietnam after police dismantled a cat meat trafficking network, with dozens of animals later reunited with their owners.

stories.title: Messi hat-trick fuels Argentina's World Cup title hopes
stories.summary: Fans in Buenos Aires celebrated as Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick to lead defending champions Argentina to a 3-0 victory over Algeria in their World Cup opener.

stories.title: Iran pledges no nuclear weapons as US commits $300bn, leaked deal draft shows
stories.summary: The full US-Iran framework deal to end the war, as circulated during the G7 summit, would see Iran abandon its pursuit of a nuclear weapon in exchange for financial incentives to comply with the agreement.

stories.title: Trump jokes ‘I’m the boss’ as he arrives for G7 meeting
stories.summary: Donald Trump joked that he was “the boss” as he arrived for a G7 meeting in France on Wednesday. The light-hearted remark came before leaders gathered for talks on trade, economic challenges and global financial stability alongside representatives from partner nations.

stories.title: Bystanders smash windows of private jet to rescue passengers after crash
stories.summary: One of the six people on board died in the crash, a local official said. The five survivors were taken to hospital and are in a stable condition.

stories.title: Israeli settlers torch mosque in West Bank village of Jiljilya
stories.summary: Residents of Jiljilya, north of Ramallah, awoke on 17 June to find their mosque set alight overnight, with burnt tyres, Hebrew graffiti reading "revenge" and extensive damage left behind in an area of the occupied West Bank where Israelis are prohibited from entering.

stories.title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.title: Netherlands hosts Japan’s emperor Naruhito to boost 400-year ties
stories.summary: The Netherlands welcomed Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako to Amsterdam on 17 June during an official visit aimed at celebrating the long-standing relationship between the two countries.

stories.title: Afghan government staff told to abandon smartphones or face prison sentence
stories.summary: How widely the rule is being implemented across Afghanistan and the scale of its impact were not entirely clear.

stories.title: Leader of South Africa's second biggest party wants his predecessor sacked as minister
stories.summary: John Steenhuisen is one of South Africa's best-known politicians after leading the DA for seven years.

stories.title: Nigerian man jailed for storing human faeces outside his home
stories.summary: Neighbours said the stench from the property had become so overwhelming that it was impossible to relax at home.

stories.summary: It remains unclear whether the 14-point draft that was circulated at G7 meetings in France will be the version formalised at a ceremony in Switzerland on Friday.

stories.summary: While violence has declined in Lebanon since a US-Iran agreement to end the Middle East war was announced on Monday, Israeli strikes on the south have killed at least five people since the deal, according to Lebanese state media.

stories.title: AI will create more jobs for humans, not replace them, Amazon founder Bezos says
stories.summary: The Amazon founder, who now has robotics and space travel companies, thinks AI will create a labour shortage.

stories.title: Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital

stories.title: Serial killer who murdered eight women in Long Island town imprisoned for life
stories.summary: In fiery exchange, judge sentenced him to three life sentences plus 25 years to life on four other charges, all running consecutively.

stories.title: FBI foils plot targeting White House UFC event, five arrested across multiple states
stories.summary: Five suspects from Ohio, Missouri and California are in custody after the FBI learned of the threat four days before the South Lawn event.

stories.title: US and Iran deal to end war allows Tehran to sell oil and fuel – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. You can follow all the latest developments from the G7 summit in our Europe live blog: We will be including any Iran-related news from the summit in our Middle East crisis live blog. Continue reading...

stories.summary: A headteacher, a motorcycle taxi driver and a travel agent are among those who are counting the human and economic cost of the virus Justin Keno watches more than 400 pupils stream through the Nelson Mandela school’s gate each morning, and wonders which of them might be carrying Ebola. stories.title: UN chief visits Haiti as gang violence soars and number of displaced hits 1.5 million
stories.summary: Guterres’ first stop was the headquarters of the new gang-suppression force, which the UN Security Council approved in September.

stories.title: The bikers battling extreme heat and armed conflict to smuggle Iranian fuel to Pakistan
stories.summary: It's so hot the fuel can catch fire - the bikers tell the BBC they must live with the risk of injury and death.

stories.title: Brazil convicts Jair Bolsonaro's son of pursuing US help in father's legal battle
stories.summary: Eduardo Bolsonaro called the conviction "baseless and senseless".

stories.title: Australian shock jock wins A$12m payout after radio station tore up contract
stories.summary: Kyle Sandilands was sacked after an on-air spat with his co-host, who accused him of bullying.

stories.title: Kazakhstan’s skies welcome Central Asia’s first Paragliding World Cup
stories.summary: Around 150 pilots from 33 countries have gathered in Kazakhstan's Zhetysu region for Central Asia's first-ever Paragliding World Cup.

stories.title: Iranian oil tankers pass US blockade ahead of peace deal signing ceremony
stories.summary: The naval blockade began on 13 April and targeted all vessels entering or departing Iranian ports and coastal areas.

stories.title: Washington's Lincoln Memorial pool turns green days after renovation
stories.summary: An algae bloom has turned Washington's newly renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool green just days after a $14 million makeover.

stories.title: Telegram challenges India ban over exam paper leak fears
stories.summary: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov has called the ban a "mistake", arguing it would punish millions of users.

stories.title: Ukraine: Russian drone attack on Zaporizhzhia home kills one, injures seven
stories.summary: At least one person was killed and seven others injured after a Russian drone struck a residential building in the southeastern Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia on 16 June, sparking a major fire and causing extensive damage, Ukrainian officials said.

stories.title: Japan raids ice cream giants over price-fixing allegations

stories.title: Kiitták Bostont sörből a skótok

stories.summary: A történelem során a legtöbb királyi sírt feldúlták, a csontokat egy osszáriumban gyűjtötték össze.

stories.summary: A közlekedési cég szerint a filmes munkálatokat azért hétvégére, az iskolai szünet idejére időzítették, mert az M2-es metró utasforgalma a hétköznapokhoz képest jelentősen alacsonyabb.

stories.title: Tényleg létezik egy titkos társaság, amit Peter Thiel milliárdos alapított, és ami most már nem is annyira titkos
stories.summary: A Dialog nevű titkos társaság tagnyilvántartása kiszivárgott egy svájci hacktivistának köszönhetően.

stories.title: Vízumot adna a spanyolok ellen mindent kivédő Vozinha anyjának az amerikai külügy
stories.summary: A spanyolok elleni meccs hőse, a zöld-fokiak kapusa a meccs után könnyeivel küszködve panaszolta fel, hogy édesanyja a vízumköltségek miatt nem tudott elutazni a világbajnokságra.

stories.summary: Hétfőn és kedden is rendkívüli parlamenti ülés lesz.

stories.summary: Nyilvánosak a képviselői mandátummal nem rendelkező miniszterek vagyonnyilatkozatai. Az igazságügyi miniszter BMW X5-össel jár, a nemzetbiztonsági főtanácsadó a Tisza Párt kampányfőnökeként milliókat keresett, az oktatási miniszter kutatóként tekintélyes megtakarításokra tett szert. Az agrárminiszter földjei 36 pótoldalra fértek rá.

stories.title: Vidékiként látja, több rendőrt akarnak az utcára az emberek – mondta az országos rendőrfőkapitány-jelölt a meghallgatásán
stories.summary: Mecser Tamás szerint kiöregedett az állomány, és mindig furcsának tartotta a tömeges bírságolást. Parlamenti bizottsági meghallgatásán kérdezték a TEK feldarabolásáról és a Bűnvadászokról is.

stories.title: A régióban már olcsóbb volt a tankolás június elején, mint itthon védett áron
stories.summary: De ez a héten akár meg is változhat, egyes kutakon ugyanis már a védett árnál olcsóbban is lehet majd tankolni, miután a piaci árak esni kezdtek.

stories.title: Megszavazta az Európai Parlament az illegális migránsok visszaküldésére vonatkozó szabályok szigorítását
stories.summary: Az új szabályokkal a visszaéléseket, és az EU-n belüli engedély nélküli mozgásokat akadályoznák meg, és megnyitnák az utat az EU-n kívüli visszaküldési központok előtt.

stories.title: A brit miniszterelnök szerint rendkívül aggasztó a La Manche csatornán figyelmeztető lövéseket leadó orosz hadihajó ügye
stories.summary: Keir Starmer arról is beszélt, hogy Oroszország napról napra egyre agresszívabban viselkedik, emiatt az európai országoknak ébernek kell lenniük.

stories.title: Matusik Tamást nevezték ki a Fővárosi Törvényszék új elnökévé
stories.summary: Az Országos Bírói Tanács volt elnökét a fideszes sajtó többször is támadta korábban.

stories.title: Házkutatást tartottak egy olasz gyanúsítottnál, aki pénzt fizethetett azért, hogy civilekre lőjön a délszláv háborúban
stories.summary: A hatóságok szerint a gyanúsítottak nagy összegeket fizettek szerb milíciáknak azért, hogy szórakozásból bosnyák civilekre lőhessenek Szarajevó ostroma alatt.

stories.title: Ügyvédjük nem érti, miért szándékos emberöléssel vádolták meg a brazil ugrótragédia vádlottjait
stories.summary: Egy 21 éves brazil nő halt bele, hogy nem ellenőrizte senki, hídugrása közben hozzá rögzítették-e a kötelet.

stories.title: Trump: Ha nem jön be a megállapodás, megint bombázhatjuk Iránt
stories.summary: Az elnök arról beszélt, hogy nem végleges az Iránnal kötött szándéknyilatkozat.

stories.title: Súlyos hibát talált a FIFA rendszerében egy etikus hekker, bármire kicserélhette volna a hivatalos kameraképeket
stories.summary: Például Subway Surfers játékmenetre, vagy valami sokkal durvábbra. A hekker mindenkinek szólt, akinek tudott, a FIFA vissza nem jelzett, de hamar orvosolta a problémát.

stories.title: Szinte teljesen bekebelezte a NER a pénztárgéppiacot, most új korszak jöhet
stories.summary: A NAV-val közvetlenül összekötött online pénztárgépek bevezetése jelentős adóbevétel-növekedést és a kiskereskedelem fehéredését hozta, de a piac az elmúlt években szűk, erősen beágyazott szereplők kezébe került.

stories.title: Akár havi 280 ezer forintos bértámogatást kaphatnak a vendéglátóhelyek idén nyáron minden diákmunkás után
stories.summary: A programba 16 és 25 év közötti fiatalok jelentkezhetnek, ehhez egy regisztrációs nyomtatványt kell kitölteniük.

stories.title: Többszörös életfogytiglanra ítélték a Gilgo Beach-i sorozatgyilkost
stories.summary: Rex Heuermannt hét gyilkosságért ítélték el, de egy nyolcadikat is bevallott.

stories.title: Portugália–Kongó 1-1
A FIFA szerint kedden megdőlt a foci-vb-k napi nézőrekordja
stories.summary: A hivatalos számok alapján összesen 281 223 néző volt kíváncsi a négy keddi meccsre.

stories.title: A Büdös-hegy adta az év talaját
stories.summary: Az erdélyi Bálványosfürdőn kiásott szelvény megmutatja a szén-dioxid- és kén-hidrogén- feláramlásokat is. A talajoknál sokszor a történet számít, nem a tudomány. Csökkentse ön is talajvakságát!

stories.title: Közel háromszorosára nőhet a szegedi fizető parkolóövezet mérete
stories.summary: Eddig nem tudtak parkolni a külvárosiak az ingázók miatt. Egy javaslat szerint 2027-től az ott élő szegedieknek is kedvezményes bérletet kell majd venniük a helyekért.

stories.title: Amerikai tisztviselők elmondták a 14 pontból álló amerikai–iráni megállapodás részleteit
stories.summary: Donald Trump szerint napokon belül alá is írhatják. A szándéknyilatkozat alapján a katonai műveleteknek azonnal véget kell vetni, valamint a Hormuzi-szorost is újranyitják.

stories.title: Luigi Mangione azzal védekezhet a tárgyalásán, hogy lelki válság idején lőtte le a UnitedHealthcare-vezért
stories.summary: Ha meggyőzi az esküdteket, gondatlanságból elkövetett emberölésben találhatják bűnösnek.

stories.title: Egy idős férfi vagyonokat érő Lego-gyűjteménye, egy mindenre elszánt youtuber és a véget nem érő PR-katasztrófa
stories.summary: Egy amerikai youtuber zászlajára tűzte, hogy visszaszerzi a ritka Star Wars-tematikájú Legókat egy oregoni bizományi boltból, és nem válogatott a módszerekben. Az egyik nagy amerikai bolthálózat Patreonig érő felszólításától kezdve sokat bírált rendőrségi fellépésig minden van az óriásbotrányban, amiben végül a youtubert vették őrizetbe.

stories.title: Hárommeccses eltiltást kapott a dél-afrikai játékos, akit egy ütés miatt állítottak ki Mexikó ellen
stories.summary: Themba Zwane odacsapott Roberto Alvaradónak, az eltiltása azt jelenti, hogy ha Dél-Afrika nem jut tovább a csoportból, nem is léphet többet pályára.

stories.title: Vb-rangadó: Anglia–Horvátország 0-0
stories.summary: Élőben a csoportkör talán legjobban várt meccse.

stories.title: Tüdőtranszplantáción esett át Mette-Marit norvég koronahercegné
stories.summary: 12 nappal a műtét előtt jelezte a palota, hogy Mette-Marit már a transzplantációs listán van.

stories.title: Toronto police link dozens of shootings to ‘multilayered’ gun-for-hire network
stories.summary: Young adults and teens are being recruited through apps like Telegram and paid to carry out attacks, officials say Police investigators in Toronto have said that dozens of shootings – including one at the US consulate in March – are linked to a “multilayered” gun-for-hire network that is also responsible for attacks on synagogues around Canada’s largest city. Toronto’s police chief, Myron Demkiw, told reporters on Tuesday that young adults and teenagers are being recruited through encrypted mess

stories.title: Musk to sue German broadcaster ZDF over 'hunt for migrants'
stories.summary: Elon Musk said he was taking legal action against one of Germany's public broadcasters after it reported that he had called for "a hunt for migrants" in Belfast.

stories.title: European Parliament approves US trade deal to lower tariffs
stories.summary: The European Parliament has approved cutting tariffs on many US imports to comply with a deal signed last year. US President Trump had given the bloc a July 4 deadline to meet its side of the deal.

stories.title: Failed joint jet project highlights Europe's defense dilemma
stories.summary: The collapse of a Franco-German fighter jet project and a faltering joint battle tank program show how national industrial interests can outweigh common defense objectives.

stories.title: 6.7 magnitude earthquake strikes off of Indonesia, US Geological Survey says
stories.summary: Authorities have urged the public to heed official advice and instructions and exercise caution, warning that aftershocks can last hours.

stories.title: Protesting and cheering as Iran's World Cup match divides diaspora
stories.summary: Iran's opening World Cup match drew both supporters and protesters outside the stadium near Los Angeles, highlighting divisions within the Iranian-American community.

stories.title: Trump tells Netanyahu 'be responsible with Lebanon, let Syria handle Hezbollah' at G7
stories.summary: Trump questions Netanyahu's methods, says Beirut strike was excessive, and points to Syria’s President as the most “capable” of countering Hezbollah.

stories.title: Russian Tu-22M3 bomber crashes during training flight
stories.summary: Russian Tu-22M3 strategic bomber has crashed in the Irkutsk region during a training flight, amid reports of engine failure. The Defence Ministry said it was not carrying weapons and the crew ejected safely. Four people were taken to hospital.

stories.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
stories.summary: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.

stories.title: Indian outrage over US killing of sailors mounts as leaders attend G7 summit
stories.summary: Relations at lowest ebb in years after Washington refuses to apologise for deaths in strait of Hormuz Fury has continued to mount in India over the US’s refusal to apologise for the deaths of Indian sailors killed in strikes in the strait of Hormuz, further straining relations between the two countries as their leaders meet at the G7 summit in France this week. Last week, three Indian seafarers, who were working on board commercial oil tankers, were killed when the US launched missile strikes on

stories.title: Russia strikes leave historic Kyiv cathedral in flames
stories.summary: The Kyiv Pechersk Lavra monastery, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was set on fire as Russia struck multiple cities in Ukraine overnight. Meanwhile, Moscow reported three deaths from a Ukrainian drone attack.

stories.title: I’ve found a way to get free ferries to France – and I can’t believe my luck | Zoe Williams
stories.summary: While taking the boat to Dunkirk, I stumbled upon a remarkable deal. I’ve barely been on dry land since In February, my sister and I took the ferry from Dover to Dunkirk. On the way back, I noticed there was a deal: spend €150 (£130) in duty free and get a free day-trip ticket. If you love Toblerone and know anyone who smokes, you can spend that without even blinking – and so it came to pass that I was back on the ferry within a month. This was when I started to perfect the art of the day trip,

stories.title: Bank of Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high … of 1%
stories.summary: Country acts amid Iran war inflation pressures, but US Fed and Bank of England expected to hold rates Business live – latest updates The Bank of Japan (BoJ) has raised interest rates to a 31-year high as it tries to dampen inflationary pressures created by the Iran war. Policymakers in Tokyo raised the BoJ’s short-term policy rate by a quarter of one percentage point, to 1% from 0.75% , and warned that companies were passing on rising oil costs to each other at a “relatively fast pace”. Continue

stories.title: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’
stories.summary: Japanese technology company at centre of Post Office IT scandal is negotiating settlement with UK government over faulty software Business live – latest updates The chair of Fujitsu, the Japanese technology firm at the centre of the Post Office IT scandal, has resigned after its board became aware of his “woman-related inappropriate conduct”. The company said on Tuesday that Hidenori Furuta had stepped down after two years in the role. Continue reading...

stories.title: Skeptical Republicans demand details of US-Iran outline peace deal
stories.summary: JD Vance says specifics ⁠to be ‌worked out as Senate Republicans say there are many unanswered questions Republicans have expressed tentative skepticism of the agreement Donald Trump has reached with Iran, and urged the White House to release more information. The memorandum of understanding (MOU) announced on Sunday to end the war in Iran, set for a ceremonial signing on Friday in Geneva, is centered around reopening the strait of Hormuz and lifting the US naval blockade in the region, along wi

stories.title: Zelenskyy’s clever play to win over Trump at G7 - The Latest
stories.summary: Donald Trump has urged Russia to ‘make a deal’ with Ukraine as the leaders of G7 countries meet on Tuesday and try to put the conflict back at the top of the agenda. European leaders are hoping to capture Trump’s attention for long enough to speak to him about Ukraine, with the US president’s focus more on the US-Israeli war against Iran. Nosheen Iqbal speaks to the Guardian’s Europe correspondent Jon Henley. Continue reading...

stories.title: Starmer’s waning influence is apparent at awkward G7 summit
stories.summary: British prime minister was left making small talk unsure if a meeting with Trump and Zelenskyy was going ahead The wait for Keir Starmer’s first session of the G7 gathering in Évian-les-Bains was undoubtedly awkward. A meeting about the future of Ukraine had been due to start at 9am but more than half an hour later, Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Emmanuel Macron were nowhere to be seen. On a live Reuters feed, Starmer could be seen standing next to the leaders of Canada and Japan as they

stories.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge opens in Paris in nod to Christo
stories.summary: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

stories.title: Charging ahead: EVs outpace growth predictions
stories.summary: Electric cars are getting cheaper, more efficient and can travel farther than ever. China is driving the transition, but Europe and other countries are catching up fast.

stories.title: World Cup: Cape Verde hero and Iran suffer US travel woes
stories.summary: After starring in Cape Verde's draw with Spain, goalkeeper Vozinha shared his sadness that an expensive travel bond stopped his mother from attending. On the same day, Iran's coach said his team were being "oppressed."

stories.title: Germany news: Population shrinks for first time since 2020
stories.summary: A dip in net migration meant it could not cancel out Germany's low birth rates in 2025. Meanwhile, fuel prices fell amid news of a possible deal between the US and Iran. And Merz gives Trump a Germany jersey. More on DW.

stories.title: Germany: 25 years since Nazi forced labor compensation began
stories.summary: A German fund set up to compensate the millions forced to work for the Nazi regime is marking 25 years since the first payments were made. But for many victims, it was much too late.

stories.title: Germany's Merz: Who are his international allies?
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has forged relations with Donald Trump, Emmanuel Macron, Keir Starmer and Giorgia Meloni. With some he is surprisingly close.

stories.title: 21-year-old woman dies after being launched off bridge without safety rope
stories.summary: Viral videos of the tragedy shared online show the anguish of onlookers when they realised the lady was launched off the Ponte do Esqueleto bridge in Brazil without the required safety rope. “She’s got no rope,” one witness to the accident is heard saying.

stories.title: 8 dead as B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards Air Force Base in California
stories.summary: A B-52 Stratofortress went down at around 11:20 a.m. local time on Monday, bursting into flames and killing everyone on board.

stories.title: 'Greatest feeling ever': Cape Verdeans tell BBC of joy at holding Spain to draw
stories.summary: The streets of the capital, Praia, shook with wild celebrations as the small island nation held Spain to a shock 0-0 draw.

stories.title: Trump appears hesitant about ally-proposed plan to demine Strait of Hormuz for commercial traffic
stories.summary: Trump appeared hesitant to accept the European proposal, stressing that the strategic waterway will become open and safe thanks to his tentative deal to end the war with Iran, but remained open to the idea of some naval presence to ensure water safety for commercial passage.

stories.title: Iranian-Americans protest against Iran team at World Cup
stories.summary: The BBC’s Shaimaa Khalil was outside the Iran v New Zealand opening round match as protesters called for an end to Tehran’s clerical regime.

stories.title: Iran 'played Trump like a violin' in deal, former national security adviser Bolton tells Euronews
stories.summary: Bolton tells Euronews the US sacrificed its strategic leverage as Trump prioritised lowering oil prices, while Iran walks away with the deal it wanted.

stories.title: Eight killed in US Air Force B-52 bomber crash in California
stories.summary: Eight people were killed when a B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after takeoff from Edwards Air Force Base in Southern California. Officials said the aircraft was on a routine test mission and the cause remains under investigation.

stories.title: Crowds flock to JR's giant cave installation on Paris' Pont Neuf
stories.summary: Visitors queued to explore French artist JR's new immersive installation, which transforms Paris' oldest bridge, the Pont Neuf, into a giant cave open around the clock.

stories.title: Hundreds of cats destined for slaughter rescued in Vietnam as police dismantle theft ring
stories.summary: Vietnamese police arrested nine people and rescued more than 400 live cats destined for slaughter, reuniting dozens with their owners. Around 100 of the animals later died from their ordeal.

stories.title: Ships start to trickle through Strait of Hormuz, but who will run it still in doubt
stories.summary: Trump said the strait would be "completely open" by Friday, but key details over who will manage the crucial waterway remain unresolved. Meanwhile, Iran has made further claims that it will control Hormuz in the future based on its interpretation of the framework deal with the US.

stories.title: Watch: California wildfires rage near passing vehicles
stories.summary: The fires in Riverside County cover over 2,000 acres, say authorities.

stories.title: UN says 2.4m refugees will need resettling in 2027 and warns of shortage of options
stories.summary: Afghan refugees are the largest group in need of resettlement, followed by people from South Sudan, Sudan, Syria and Rohingya refugees from Myanmar, living in vast camps in Bangladesh.

errors.error: <urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
<urlopen error [Errno -2] Name or service not known>
